flags.notes: This scene has NO EYES — that is expected and correct, not a problem with the image. Do not look for eyes. Just redraw the drawing as a chalk line drawing, whitening only small naturally-white features if there are any.

chalk.why: The eye-flavored chalk instruction made the model REFUSE the eyeless scene ('there are no eyes in the image to edit') — a --notes saying the eyeless scene is expected fixed it in one pass (commits 46daba9 + 6f6e223; pipeline.md status-table lesson). Note text reconstructed from that prose.
First pass failed on local drift; 'passed at -t 0.3 with a bigger attempt budget' (commit 6f6e223).

flags.notes: The scene includes a STAR above the teddy — keep it exactly where it is. Redraw only the solid eye pupils as thin outlined rings; change nothing else.

normalize.why: Idea-run #6: the first normalize attempt DELETED the star (worst-tile keep 0%) — the bee-wide deleted-cloud precedent; a motif-naming --notes fixed it on the retry.

chalk.review: Expect a worst-tile keep FAIL (~75%) confined to the solid pen pupils the chalk deliberately whitens into sclera + outlined pupil — the keep-gate blind spot (commit 4fff334). Verify on the overlay that the miss is only the whitened eyes, then ship the generator's candidate byte-for-byte.
chalk.why: commit 4fff334: best-of-6 at -t 0.3 failed only worst-tile keep 75.4% vs 80%, shipped after overlay review.

night.why: Needed the strict-gate retry: '--line-white-min 175 -t 0.3, lineW 94 -> 255' (commit dc20072); max-attempts from pipeline.md Stage 4 lever 1's pairing.
